Imaginary (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $28,009,161Details
International Box Office $15,664,455
Worldwide Box Office $43,673,616
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $794,819
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,135,769
Total $1,930,588
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When Jessica moves back into her childhood home with her family, her youngest stepdaughter Alice develops an eerie attachment to a stuffed bear named Chauncey she finds in the basement. Alice starts playing games with Chauncey that begin playful and become increasingly sinister. As Alice’s behavior becomes more and more concerning, Jessica intervenes only to realize Chauncey is much more than the stuffed toy bear she believed him to be.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,912,713 (35.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.83 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$13,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,118 opening theaters/3,118 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $29,618,880

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 8th, 2024 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: March 6th, 2024 (Wide) (France)
March 6th, 2024 (Wide) (Belgium)
March 7th, 2024 (Wide), released as Imaginary (ΕΝΑΣ ΦΑΝΤΑΣΤΙΚΟΣ ΦΙΛΟΣ) (Greece)
March 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Netherlands)
March 7th, 2024 (Wide), released as Imaginario (Argentina)
Video Release: March 26th, 2024 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violent content, drug material and language.
(Rating bulletin 2820 (cert #54729), 2/7/2024)
Running Time: 104 minutes
Filming location (city or town): New Orleans, LA
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Louisiana, United States
Plot point: Living Toys, Step-Family, You Can't Go Home Again
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Supernatural Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, Lionsgate, Tower of Babble
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
